The reason for this strong response is that (in this extreme setting) all of the selection is on the b"

label: Formula 20.15c
section: Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction
subsection: Complications in the Absence of Environmental Change
nearby_text: fluencing fitness and our focal character is through shared environmental effects, giving $ \beta_E \neq 0 $, while $ \beta_A = \beta_z = 0 $. In this case, using Equation 20.9b, the response becomes $$ R=\beta_{E}\sigma(E,A)=S\left(\frac{\sigma(E,A)}{\sigma_{E}^{2}+\sigma(E,A)}\right) $$ (20.15c) which equals zero unless a genotype-environment correlation exists. Again, in the absence of a perfect correlation between E and A, this response is transient (Chapter 15).

  "nearby_text": "fluencing fitness and our focal character is through shared environmental effects, giving $ \\beta_E \\neq 0 $, while $ \\beta_A = \\beta_z = 0 $. In this case, using Equation 20.9b, the response becomes $$ R=\\beta_{E}\\sigma(E,A)=S\\left(\\frac{\\sigma(E,A)}{\\sigma_{E}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)}\\right) $$ (20.15c) which equals zero unless a genotype-environment correlation exists. Again, in the absence of a perfect correlation between E and A, this response is transient (Chapter 15)."
}

label: Formula 20.16a
section: IS A FOCAL TRAIT THE DIRECT TARGET OF SELECTION?
subsection: Robertson Consistency Tests
nearby_text: Under what conditions should the breeder’s equation and Robertson’s theorem agree? If we use the more general, 1966, version of Robertson’s theorem, the two predicted responses are equal when $$ R=h^{2}S=\frac{\sigma^{2}(A_{z})}{\sigma^{2}(z)}\sigma(z,w)=\sigma(A_{z},w) $$ (20.16a)

  "nearby_text": "Under what conditions should the breeder’s equation and Robertson’s theorem agree? label: Formula 20.19b
section: IS A FOCAL TRAIT THE DIRECT TARGET OF SELECTION?
subsection: Rausher's Consistency Criteria
nearby_text: )$, we can relate the slope, $b_z$, of the univariate regression of fitness on $z$ with the corresponding univariate regression slopes $b_A$ and $b_E$ (based on $A$ and $E$, respectively) as follows: $$ \begin{aligned}b_{z}=\frac{\sigma(w,z)}{\sigma^{2}(z)}&=\frac{\sigma(w,A)+\sigma(w,E)}{\sigma^{2}(z)}\\&=\frac{\sigma(w,A)}{\sigma^{2}(A)}h^{2}+\frac{\sigma(w,E)}{\sigma^{2}(E)}(1-h^{2})\\&=b_{A}h^{2}+b_{E}(1-h^{2})\\ \end{aligned} $$ (20.19b)

nearby_text: e Rausher-Simms equality test (Rausher and Simms 1989; Rausher 1992). Here, one computes the multiple regression of fitness on both the trait breeding value, A, and the residual deviation, E, namely, $$ w=1+\beta_{A}A+\beta_{E}E+e $$ (20.20)

        "section": "IS A FOCAL TRAIT THE DIRECT TARGET OF SELECTION?",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.19b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}b_{z}=\\frac{\\sigma(w,z)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)+\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}\\\\&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}h^{2}+\\frac{\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(E)}(1-h^{2})\\\\&=b_{A}h^{2}+b_{E}(1-h^{2})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.19b",
        "section": "IS A FOCAL TRAIT THE DIRECT TARGET OF SELECTION?",

        "subsection": "Rausher's Consistency Criteria",
        "position": 48,

        "latex": "\\frac{\\sigma(A_{z},A_{w})+\\sigma(E_{z},e_{w})+\\sigma(A_{z},e_{w})+\\sigma(E_{z},A_{w})}{\\sigma^{2}(A_{z})+\\sigma^{2}(E_{z})}=\\frac{\\sigma(A_{z},A_{w})+\\sigma(A_{z},e_{w})}{\\sigma^{2}(A_{z})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.21a",
        "section": "IS A FOCAL TRAIT THE DIRECT TARGET OF SELECTION?",
        "subsection": "Morrissey et al.'s Consistency Criteria",
        "position": 49,
        "symbols_defined": [

  "nearby_text": "Finally, this rearranges to yield an alternative consistency condition $$ \\frac{\\sigma(E_{z},e_{w})+\\sigma(E_{z},A_{w})}{\\sigma^{2}(E_{z})}=\\frac{\\sigma(A_{z},A_{w})+\\sigma(A_{z},e_{w})}{\\sigma^{2}(A_{z})} $$ (20.21b)"
}

label: Formula 20.21c
section: IS A FOCAL TRAIT THE DIRECT TARGET OF SELECTION?
subsection: Morrissey et al.'s Consistency Criteria
nearby_text: By assuming Robertson’s 1968 version, with $ \sigma(A_{z}, e_{w}) = 0 $, and also that $ \sigma(E_{z}, A_{w}) = 0 $, this reduces to $$ \frac{\sigma(E_{z},e_{w})}{\sigma^{2}(E_{z})}=\frac{\sigma(A_{z},A_{w})}{\sigma^{2}(A_{z})} $$ (20.21c) which is the Morrissey consistency condition (Morrissey et al. 2010, 2012).

        "id": "formula_20.19b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}b_{z}=\\frac{\\sigma(w,z)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)+\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}\\\\&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}h^{2}+\\frac{\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(E)}(1-h^{2})\\\\&=b_{A}h^{2}+b_{E}(1-h^{2})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

        "label": "Formula 20.21a",
        "section": "IS A FOCAL TRAIT THE DIRECT TARGET OF SELECTION?",

        "position": 49,

        ]

      {
        "id": "formula_20.21b",
        "latex": "\\frac{\\sigma(E_{z},e_{w})+\\sigma(E_{z},A_{w})}{\\sigma^{2}(E_{z})}=\\frac{\\sigma(A_{z},A_{w})+\\sigma(A_{z},e_{w})}{\\sigma^{2}(A_{z})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.21b",
        "section": "IS A FOCAL TRAIT THE DIRECT TARGET OF SELECTION?",
        "subsection": "Morrissey et al.'s Consistency Criteria",
        "position": 50,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "A",
          "E",
          "\\sigma",
          "e",
          "w",
          "z"
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "nearby_text": "By assuming Robertson’s 1968 version, with $ \\sigma(A_{z}, e_{w}) = 0 $, and also that $ \\sigma(E_{z}, A_{w}) = 0 $, this reduces to $$ \\frac{\\sigma(E_{z},e_{w})}{\\sigma^{2}(E_{z})}=\\frac{\\sigma(A_{z},A_{w})}{\\sigma^{2}(A_{z})} $$ (20.21c) which is the Morrissey consistency condition (Morrissey et al. 2010, 2012)."
}

label: Formula 20.22a
section: Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction
subsection: APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS: BASICS
nearby_text: In particular, the general animal model, so called because of its initial focus on estimating the breeding values of a single trait in a collection of individual animals (originally in dairy cattle), $$ \mathbf{y}=\mathbf{X}\boldsymbol{\beta}+\mathbf{Z}_{a}\mathbf{a}+\sum_{i=1}^{k}\mathbf{Z}_{i}\mathbf{u}_{i}+\mathbf{e} $$ (20.22a) has been widely used in animal breeding since the 1970s. As detailed in Chapter 19, $ \beta $ is the vector of unknown fixed effects, a is the random vector of breeding values, e is the random vector

As detailed in Chapter 19, $ \\beta $ is the vector of unknown fixed effects, a is the random vector of breeding values, e is the random vector"

        "latex": "\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma(w,z)\\\\\\sigma(w,A)\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}S\\\\\\boldsymbol{R}\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma_{z}^{2}&\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma(z,E)\\\\\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma_{A}^{2}&\\sigma(E,A)\\\\\\sigma(z,E)&\\sigma(E,A)&\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}\\beta_{z}\\\\\\beta_{A}\\\\\\beta_{E}\\end{pmatrix}",
        "label": "Formula 20.8a",

section: Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction
subsection: Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data
nearby_text: individuals, x and y. We denote the two alleles in x by a and b (which may be alike in state), and similarly in y by c and d. The molecular similarity at locus $ \ell $ between x and y is defined by $$ S_{xy,\ell}=\frac{I_{ac}+I_{ad}+I_{bc}+I_{bd}}{4} $$ (20.23a) where $ I_{ad} $ is an indicator function that equals one if $ a $ and $ d $ are AIS, and otherwise is zero. For diallelic loci (such as most SNPs), $ S_{xy,\ell} $ takes on values of 0, 1/2, or 1. A

  "nearby_text": "individuals, x and y. We denote the two alleles in x by a and b (which may be alike in state), and similarly in y by c and d. The molecular similarity at locus $ \\ell $ between x and y is defined by $$ S_{xy,\\ell}=\\frac{I_{ac}+I_{ad}+I_{bc}+I_{bd}}{4} $$ (20.23a) where $ I_{ad} $ is an indicator function that equals one if $ a $ and $ d $ are AIS, and otherwise is zero. For diallelic loci (such as most SNPs), $ S_{xy,\\ell} $ takes on values of 0, 1/2, or 1. A"
}

label: Formula 20.23b
section: Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction
subsection: Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data
nearby_text: to Equation 20.23a as molecular coancestry, as when AIS equals IBD, then $ E[S_{xy,\ell}] = \theta_{xy} $, with the average over all loci giving an estimate of the elements of the relationship matrix $$ \widehat{A}_{x y}=2\widehat{\theta}_{x y}=\frac{2}{L}\sum_{\ell=1}^{L}S_{x y,\ell} $$ (20.23b)

label: Formula 20.23c
section: Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction
subsection: Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data
nearby_text: in the base population are AIS. Obviously, $ s_\ell $ is (at a minimum) a function of the allele frequencies at $ \ell $. As shown by Lynch (1988c), the expected value for $ S_{xy,\ell} $ is given by $$ E[S_{x y,\ell}]=\theta_{x y}+(1-\theta_{x y})s_{\ell} $$ (20.23c)

label: Formula 20.23f
section: Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction
subsection: Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data
nearby_text: Summing Equation 20.23c over all loci yields $$ E[S_{xy}]=\sum_{\ell=1}^{L}E[S_{xy,\ell}]=\sum_{\ell=1}^{L}[\theta_{xy}+(1-\theta_{xy})s_\ell]=L\theta_{xy}+(1-\theta_{xy})\sum_{\ell=1}^{L}s_\ell $$ (20.23f)

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}b_{z}=\\frac{\\sigma(w,z)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)+\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}\\\\&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}h^{2}+\\frac{\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(E)}(1-h^{2})\\\\&=b_{A}h^{2}+b_{E}(1-h^{2})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

label: Formula 20.23h
section: Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction

nearby_text: p_{\ell}$ is the frequency of allele 1, and $\sigma^{2}(b_{\ell}) = p_{\ell}(1 - p_{\ell})$.   "nearby_text": "p_{\\ell}$ is the frequency of allele 1, and $\\sigma^{2}(b_{\\ell}) = p_{\\ell}(1 - p_{\\ell})$. Analogous to Equation 20.23c, the probability that a randomly drawn allele from $x$ and $y$ are both 1 is $$ \\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2} $$ (20.23h) which rearranges to $$ \\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell"
}

label: Formula 20.23i
section: Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction
subsection: Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data
nearby_text: n 20.23c, the probability that a randomly drawn allele from $x$ and $y$ are both 1 is $$ \Pr(b_{x,\ell}=b_{y,\ell}=1)=\theta_{x y}p_{\ell}+(1-\theta_{x y})p_{\ell}^{2} $$ (20.23h) which rearranges to $$ \theta_{xy}=\frac{\Pr(b_{x,\ell}=b_{y,\ell}=1)-p_{\ell}^{2}}{p_{\ell}(1-p_{\ell})}=\frac{E[b_{x,\ell}b_{y,\ell}]-(E[b_{\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\ell}(1-p_{\ell})}=\frac{\sigma(b_{x,\ell},b_{y,\ell})}{\sigma(b_{x,\ell})\sigma(b_{y,\ell})}=\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\ell},b_{y,\ell}) $$ (20.23i) namely, the correlation between a random allele in x and a random allele in y.

        "latex": "\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma(w,z)\\\\\\sigma(w,A)\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}S\\\\\\boldsymbol{R}\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma_{z}^{2}&\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma(z,E)\\\\\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma_{A}^{2}&\\sigma(E,A)\\\\\\sigma(z,E)&\\sigma(E,A)&\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}\\beta_{z}\\\\\\beta_{A}\\\\\\beta_{E}\\end{pmatrix}",

        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{x y}=2\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y}=\\frac{2}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}S_{x y,\\ell}",

        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23h",

  },
  "nearby_text": "n 20.23c, the probability that a randomly drawn allele from $x$ and $y$ are both 1 is $$ \\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2} $$ (20.23h) which rearranges to $$ \\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell}) $$ (20.23i) namely, the correlation between a random allele in x and a random allele in y."
}

label: Formula 20.23j
section: Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction

nearby_text: 0^{2}\cdot(1-p_{\ell})^{2}+1^{2}\cdot2\cdot p_{\ell}(1-p_{\ell})+2^{2}\cdot p_{\ell}^{2}\right]-(2p_{\ell})^{2}=2p_{\ell}(1-p_{\ell}) $$ yielding a correlation in T between x and y at SNP $ \ell $ of $$ \mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\ell},T_{y,\ell})=\frac{\sigma(T_{x,\ell},T_{y,\ell})}{\sigma(T_{x,\ell})\cdot\sigma(T_{y,\ell})}=\frac{(T_{x,\ell}-2p_{\ell})\left(T_{y,\ell}-2p_{\ell}\right)}{2p_{\ell}(1-p_{\ell})} $$ (20.23j)

        "label": "Formula 20.23i",

}

section: Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction

nearby_text: Substituting this result into Equation 20.23j and recalling Equation 20.23i yields $$ \mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\ell},T_{y,\ell})=\frac{4\sigma(b_{x,\ell},b_{y,\ell})}{2p_{\ell}(1-p_{\ell})}=2\left[\frac{\sigma(b_{x,\ell},b_{y,\ell})}{p_{\ell}(1-p_{\ell})}\right]=2\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\ell},b_{y,\ell})=2\theta_{xy} $$ (20.23k)

      {
        "id": "formula_20.8a",
        "latex": "\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma(w,z)\\\\\\sigma(w,A)\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}S\\\\\\boldsymbol{R}\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma_{z}^{2}&\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma(z,E)\\\\\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma_{A}^{2}&\\sigma(E,A)\\\\\\sigma(z,E)&\\sigma(E,A)&\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}\\beta_{z}\\\\\\beta_{A}\\\\\\beta_{E}\\end{pmatrix}",

        "latex": "\\sigma(z,A)=\\sigma(A+E+e_{s},A)=\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.9a",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}S&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma(z,A)\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(z,E)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\left[\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{A}+\\left[\\sigma_{E}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}+\\sigma(E,A)(\\beta_{A}+\\beta_{E})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

        "subsection": "Complications in the Absence of Environmental Change",

        "id": "formula_20.9b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R&=\\sigma(A,z)\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\left[\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)(\\beta_{z}+\\beta_{E})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

        "latex": "h^{2}\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{z}=h^{2}\\left[S-\\left(\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}\\right)\\right]",

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}b_{z}=\\frac{\\sigma(w,z)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)+\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}\\\\&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}h^{2}+\\frac{\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(E)}(1-h^{2})\\\\&=b_{A}h^{2}+b_{E}(1-h^{2})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{x y}=2\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y}=\\frac{2}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}S_{x y,\\ell}",

      {

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23h",
        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23i",
        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",
        "label": "Formula 20.23i",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23j",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",

        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",

        ]
      }

  },
  "nearby_text": "Substituting this result into Equation 20.23j and recalling Equation 20.23i yields $$ \\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{4\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=2\\left[\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\right]=2\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})=2\\theta_{xy} $$ (20.23k)"

        "id": "formula_20.19b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}b_{z}=\\frac{\\sigma(w,z)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)+\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}\\\\&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}h^{2}+\\frac{\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(E)}(1-h^{2})\\\\&=b_{A}h^{2}+b_{E}(1-h^{2})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

      {

        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{x y}=2\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y}=\\frac{2}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}S_{x y,\\ell}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23b",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",

        "latex": "E[S_{x y,\\ell}]=\\theta_{x y}+(1-\\theta_{x y})s_{\\ell}",

      {

        "latex": "E[S_{xy}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}E[S_{xy,\\ell}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}[\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})s_\\ell]=L\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_\\ell",
        "label": "Formula 20.23f",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23h",
        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23h",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 62,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23i",
        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",
        "label": "Formula 20.23i",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 63,
        "symbols_defined": [

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23j",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23j",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 64,
        "symbols_defined": [

          "x",
          "y"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23k",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{4\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=2\\left[\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\right]=2\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})=2\\theta_{xy}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23k",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 65,
        "symbols_defined": [

Note that this term could be negative, implying that the individual shows less homozygosity than expected (Ackerman et al. 2017). Two t"

nearby_text: Summing over uncorrelated SNPs returns $$ \widehat{A}_{xy}=2\widehat{\theta}_{xy}=\frac{1}{L}\sum_{\ell=1}^{L}\frac{\left(T_{x,\ell}-2p_{\ell}\right)\left(T_{y,\ell}-2p_{\ell}\right)}{2p_{\ell}(1-p_{\ell})}\quad for x\neq y $$ (20.231)

        "latex": "\\widehat{\\theta}_{xy}=\\frac{1}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}\\frac{S_{xy,\\ell}-s_{\\ell}}{1-s_{\\ell}}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23d",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",

        "position": 58,
        "symbols_defined": [

        "latex": "S_{xy}=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}S_{xy,\\ell}",

        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 59,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23f",
        "latex": "E[S_{xy}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}E[S_{xy,\\ell}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}[\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})s_\\ell]=L\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_\\ell",
        "label": "Formula 20.23f",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23g",
        "latex": "\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y,D W}=\\frac{S_{x y}-\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_{\\ell}}{L-\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_{\\ell}}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23g",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 61,
        "symbols_defined": [

          "x",
          "y"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23h",
        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23h",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 62,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\Pr",
          "b",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23i",
        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",
        "label": "Formula 20.23i",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 63,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\theta",
          "x",
          "y"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23j",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23j",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 64,
        "symbols_defined": [

          "x",
          "y"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23k",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{4\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=2\\left[\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\right]=2\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})=2\\theta_{xy}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23k",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 65,
        "symbols_defined": [

          "\\mathrm{r}",
          "x",
          "y"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23m",
        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{x x}=1+\\frac{1}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}\\frac{T_{x,\\ell}^{2}-(1+2p_{\\ell})T_{x,\\ell}+2p_{\\ell}^{2}}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23m",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 66,
        "symbols_defined": [

}

label: Formula 20.24a
section: APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS
subsection: Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs
nearby_text: , $ A $ and the residual $ \epsilon $ are uncorrelated, implying $ \sigma(\widehat{a}, A) = \sigma(\widehat{a}, \widehat{a} + \epsilon) = \sigma^2(\widehat{a}) $. This allows us to express the PEV as $$ \sigma^{2}(\epsilon)=\sigma^{2}(\widehat{a}-A)=\sigma^{2}(\widehat{a})-2\sigma(\widehat{a},A)+\sigma^{2}(A)=\sigma^{2}(A)-\sigma^{2}(\widehat{a}) $$ (20.24a)

  "nearby_text": ", $ A $ and the residual $ \\epsilon $ are uncorrelated, implying $ \\sigma(\\widehat{a}, A) = \\sigma(\\widehat{a}, \\widehat{a} + \\epsilon) = \\sigma^2(\\widehat{a}) $. This allows us to express the PEV as $$ \\sigma^{2}(\\epsilon)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a}-A)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})-2\\sigma(\\widehat{a},A)+\\sigma^{2}(A)=\\sigma^{2}(A)-\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a}) $$ (20.24a)"
}

label: Formula 20.24b

        "latex": "\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma(w,z)\\\\\\sigma(w,A)\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}S\\\\\\boldsymbol{R}\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma_{z}^{2}&\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma(z,E)\\\\\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma_{A}^{2}&\\sigma(E,A)\\\\\\sigma(z,E)&\\sigma(E,A)&\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}\\beta_{z}\\\\\\beta_{A}\\\\\\beta_{E}\\end{pmatrix}",

        "id": "formula_20.9a",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}S&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma(z,A)\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(z,E)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\left[\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{A}+\\left[\\sigma_{E}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}+\\sigma(E,A)(\\beta_{A}+\\beta_{E})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

label: Formula 20.24d
section: APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS
subsection: Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs
nearby_text: he correlation between the PBV and the phenotypic value of the trait, z, is greater than the correlation between A and z, as $ \sigma(\widehat{a}, z) = \sigma(A - \epsilon, z) = \sigma(A, z) $, hence $$ \begin{aligned}\left|\operatorname{corr}(\widehat{a},z)\right|&=\frac{\left|\sigma(\widehat{a},z)\right|}{\sqrt{\sigma^{2}(\widehat{a})\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\frac{\left|\sigma(A,z)\right|}{\sqrt{\sigma^{2}(\widehat{a})\sigma^{2}(z)}}\\&=\frac{\left|\sigma(A,z)\right|}{\rho\sqrt{\sigma^{2}(A)\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\frac{\left|\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\right|}{\rho}\geq\left|\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\right|\end{aligned} $$ (20.24d) where we use corr to denote a correlation, as $ \rho $ here is restricted to a very specific correlation, namely, that between $ \hat{a} $ and $ A $ (the accuracy). Equations 20.24a and 20.24c show t

        "id": "formula_20.8b",
        "latex": "\\sigma(z,A)=\\sigma(A+E+e_{s},A)=\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)",

        "section": "MODIFYING THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FOR NATURAL POPULATIONS",

        "position": 18,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.9a",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}S&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma(z,A)\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(z,E)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\left[\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{A}+\\left[\\sigma_{E}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}+\\sigma(E,A)(\\beta_{A}+\\beta_{E})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.9a",
        "section": "MODIFYING THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FOR NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Complications in the Absence of Environmental Change",
        "position": 19,
        "symbols_defined": [

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.9b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R&=\\sigma(A,z)\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\left[\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)(\\beta_{z}+\\beta_{E})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.9b",
        "section": "MODIFYING THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FOR NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Complications in the Absence of Environmental Change",

        "latex": "\\sigma(z_{1},z_{2})\\beta_{2}=\\sigma(A+E,z_{2})\\beta_{2}=\\sigma(A,z_{2})\\beta_{2}+\\sigma(E,z_{2})\\beta_{2}",

        "latex": "\\sigma(A,z_{2})\\beta_{2}=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}\\qquad\\mathrm{a n d}\\qquad\\sigma(E,z_{2})\\beta_{2}=\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}",

      {

        "latex": "h^{2}\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{z}=h^{2}\\left[S-\\left(\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}\\right)\\right]",

        "latex": "R=h^{2}S+\\sigma_{A}^{2}(1-h^{2})\\beta_{A}-h^{2}\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}+\\sigma(E,A)\\left[\\beta_{z}-h^{2}\\beta_{A}+(1-h^{2})\\beta_{E}\\right]",

        "symbols_defined": [

      {
        "id": "formula_20.19b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}b_{z}=\\frac{\\sigma(w,z)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)+\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}\\\\&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}h^{2}+\\frac{\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(E)}(1-h^{2})\\\\&=b_{A}h^{2}+b_{E}(1-h^{2})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

      },
      {

        "latex": "\\frac{\\sigma(A_{z},A_{w})+\\sigma(E_{z},e_{w})+\\sigma(A_{z},e_{w})+\\sigma(E_{z},A_{w})}{\\sigma^{2}(A_{z})+\\sigma^{2}(E_{z})}=\\frac{\\sigma(A_{z},A_{w})+\\sigma(A_{z},e_{w})}{\\sigma^{2}(A_{z})}",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.22a",
        "latex": "\\mathbf{y}=\\mathbf{X}\\boldsymbol{\\beta}+\\mathbf{Z}_{a}\\mathbf{a}+\\sum_{i=1}^{k}\\mathbf{Z}_{i}\\mathbf{u}_{i}+\\mathbf{e}",

        "latex": "\\begin{pmatrix}{{{\\mathbf{a}}}} \\\\{{{\\mathbf{e}}}}\\end{pmatrix}\\sim\\begin{pmatrix}{{{\\mathbf{0}}}} \\\\{{{\\mathbf{0}}}}\\end{pmatrix},\\begin{pmatrix}{{{\\sigma^{2}(A)\\mathbf{A}}}}&{{{\\mathbf{0}}}} \\\\{{{\\mathbf{0}}}}&{{{\\sigma_{e}^{2}\\mathbf{I}}}}\\end{pmatrix}",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23b",
        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{x y}=2\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y}=\\frac{2}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}S_{x y,\\ell}",

        "latex": "E[S_{x y,\\ell}]=\\theta_{x y}+(1-\\theta_{x y})s_{\\ell}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23c",

        "latex": "E[S_{xy}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}E[S_{xy,\\ell}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}[\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})s_\\ell]=L\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_\\ell",

        "latex": "\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y,D W}=\\frac{S_{x y}-\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_{\\ell}}{L-\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_{\\ell}}",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23h",
        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23i",
        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",
        "label": "Formula 20.23i",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 63,
        "symbols_defined": [

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23j",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23j",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",

        "symbols_defined": [

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23k",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{4\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=2\\left[\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\right]=2\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})=2\\theta_{xy}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23k",

  "nearby_text": "he correlation between the PBV and the phenotypic value of the trait, z, is greater than the correlation between A and z, as $ \\sigma(\\widehat{a}, z) = \\sigma(A - \\epsilon, z) = \\sigma(A, z) $, hence $$ \\begin{aligned}\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}\\\\&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(A)\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho}\\geq\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|\\end{aligned} $$ (20.24d) where we use corr to denote a correlation, as $ \\rho $ here is restricted to a very specific correlation, namely, that between $ \\hat{a} $ and $ A $ (the accuracy). Equations 20.24a and 20.24c show t"
}

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23h",
        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",

        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23i",
        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",
        "label": "Formula 20.23i",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 63,
        "symbols_defined": [

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23j",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23j",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 64,
        "symbols_defined": [

          "x",
          "y"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23k",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{4\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=2\\left[\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\right]=2\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})=2\\theta_{xy}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23k",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",

        "symbols_defined": [

        "label": "Formula 20.23m",

        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{xy}=2\\widehat{\\theta}_{xy}=\\frac{1}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}\\frac{\\left(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\quad for x\\neq y",
        "label": "Formula 20.231",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.24a",
        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}(\\epsilon)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a}-A)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})-2\\sigma(\\widehat{a},A)+\\sigma^{2}(A)=\\sigma^{2}(A)-\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})",
        "label": "Formula 20.24a",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",
        "position": 68,
        "symbols_defined": [

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24b",
        "latex": "\\rho=\\frac{\\sigma(\\widehat{a},A)}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(A)}}=\\sqrt{\\frac{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}}",
        "label": "Formula 20.24b",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",
        "position": 69,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\rho"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24c",
        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})=\\rho^{2}\\sigma^{2}(A)",
        "label": "Formula 20.24c",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",
        "position": 70,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\sigma",
          "\\widehat{a}"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24d",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}\\\\&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(A)\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho}\\geq\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.24d",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",
        "position": 71,
        "symbols_defined": [

          "r",
          "z"
        ]
      }

  },
  "nearby_text": "\\rho $ here is restricted to a very specific correlation, namely, that between $ \\hat{a} $ and $ A $ (the accuracy). Equations 20.24a and 20.24c show the connection between $ \\rho $ and the PEV, with $$ \\sigma^{2}(\\epsilon)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a}-A)=\\left(1-\\rho^{2}\\right)\\sigma^{2}(A) $$ (20.24e)"
}

        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{x y}=2\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y}=\\frac{2}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}S_{x y,\\ell}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23b",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23c",
        "latex": "E[S_{x y,\\ell}]=\\theta_{x y}+(1-\\theta_{x y})s_{\\ell}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23c",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23d",
        "latex": "\\widehat{\\theta}_{xy}=\\frac{1}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}\\frac{S_{xy,\\ell}-s_{\\ell}}{1-s_{\\ell}}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23d",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 58,
        "symbols_defined": [

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23e",
        "latex": "S_{xy}=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}S_{xy,\\ell}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23e",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 59,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "S",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23f",
        "latex": "E[S_{xy}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}E[S_{xy,\\ell}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}[\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})s_\\ell]=L\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_\\ell",
        "label": "Formula 20.23f",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23g",
        "latex": "\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y,D W}=\\frac{S_{x y}-\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_{\\ell}}{L-\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_{\\ell}}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23g",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 61,
        "symbols_defined": [

          "\\widehat{\\theta}",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23h",
        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23h",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 62,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\Pr",
          "b",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23i",
        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",
        "label": "Formula 20.23i",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 63,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\theta",
          "x",
          "y"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23j",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23j",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 64,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "T",
          "\\mathrm{c}",
          "\\mathrm{o}",
          "\\mathrm{r}",
          "x",
          "y"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23k",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{4\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=2\\left[\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\right]=2\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})=2\\theta_{xy}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23k",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 65,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "T",
          "\\mathrm{c}",
          "\\mathrm{o}",
          "\\mathrm{r}",
          "x",
          "y"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23m",
        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{x x}=1+\\frac{1}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}\\frac{T_{x,\\ell}^{2}-(1+2p_{\\ell})T_{x,\\ell}+2p_{\\ell}^{2}}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23m",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 66,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\widehat{A}",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.231",
        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{xy}=2\\widehat{\\theta}_{xy}=\\frac{1}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}\\frac{\\left(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\quad for x\\neq y",
        "label": "Formula 20.231",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 67,
        "symbols_defined": [

          "x",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24a",
        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}(\\epsilon)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a}-A)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})-2\\sigma(\\widehat{a},A)+\\sigma^{2}(A)=\\sigma^{2}(A)-\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})",
        "label": "Formula 20.24a",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",
        "position": 68,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\epsilon",
          "\\sigma"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24b",
        "latex": "\\rho=\\frac{\\sigma(\\widehat{a},A)}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(A)}}=\\sqrt{\\frac{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}}",
        "label": "Formula 20.24b",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",
        "position": 69,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\rho"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24c",
        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})=\\rho^{2}\\sigma^{2}(A)",
        "label": "Formula 20.24c",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",
        "position": 70,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\sigma",
          "\\widehat{a}"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24d",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}\\\\&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(A)\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho}\\geq\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.24d",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",
        "position": 71,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\operatorname",
          "\\widehat{a}",
          "c",
          "o",
          "r",
          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24e",
        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}(\\epsilon)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a}-A)=\\left(1-\\rho^{2}\\right)\\sigma^{2}(A)",
        "label": "Formula 20.24e",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",
        "position": 72,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\epsilon",
          "\\sigma"
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "nearby_text": "○ Predicted breeding value ( $ \\rho^{2}=h^{2} $) $$ \\sigma^{2}(\\epsilon)=\\sigma(\\widehat{a},E)=\\left(1-\\rho^{2}\\right)\\sigma^{2}(A) $$ (20.24f)"
}

label: Formula 20.24g
section: Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction
subsection: Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs
nearby_text: e PEVs for each of the estimated breeding values (also see Meyer 1989; Tosh and Wilton 1994). The ith diagonal element of this covariance matrix yields a prediction-error variance for individual i of $$ PEV_{ii}=(1-\rho_{i}^{2})\sigma^{2}(A) $$ (20.24g) and hence a prediction reliability of $$ \rho_{i}^{2}=1-\left(\frac{P E V_{ii}}{\sigma^{2}(A)}\right) $$ (20.24h)

        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23h",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 62,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\Pr",
          "b",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23i",
        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",
        "label": "Formula 20.23i",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 63,
        "symbols_defined": [

        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23j",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",

        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{4\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=2\\left[\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\right]=2\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})=2\\theta_{xy}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23k",

        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}(\\epsilon)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a}-A)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})-2\\sigma(\\widehat{a},A)+\\sigma^{2}(A)=\\sigma^{2}(A)-\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})",
        "label": "Formula 20.24a",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",

        "label": "Formula 20.24b",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",

        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})=\\rho^{2}\\sigma^{2}(A)",

        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24d",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}\\\\&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(A)\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho}\\geq\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.24d",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",

        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",

        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.8a",
        "latex": "\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma(w,z)\\\\\\sigma(w,A)\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}S\\\\\\boldsymbol{R}\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma_{z}^{2}&\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma(z,E)\\\\\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma_{A}^{2}&\\sigma(E,A)\\\\\\sigma(z,E)&\\sigma(E,A)&\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}\\beta_{z}\\\\\\beta_{A}\\\\\\beta_{E}\\end{pmatrix}",

        "latex": "\\sigma(z,A)=\\sigma(A+E+e_{s},A)=\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.9a",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}S&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma(z,A)\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(z,E)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\left[\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{A}+\\left[\\sigma_{E}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}+\\sigma(E,A)(\\beta_{A}+\\beta_{E})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

        "subsection": "Complications in the Absence of Environmental Change",

        "symbols_defined": [

        "id": "formula_20.9b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R&=\\sigma(A,z)\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\left[\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)(\\beta_{z}+\\beta_{E})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

        "latex": "h^{2}\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{z}=h^{2}\\left[S-\\left(\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}\\right)\\right]",

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}b_{z}=\\frac{\\sigma(w,z)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)+\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}\\\\&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}h^{2}+\\frac{\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(E)}(1-h^{2})\\\\&=b_{A}h^{2}+b_{E}(1-h^{2})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{x y}=2\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y}=\\frac{2}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}S_{x y,\\ell}",

      {

        "latex": "\\widehat{\\theta}_{xy}=\\frac{1}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}\\frac{S_{xy,\\ell}-s_{\\ell}}{1-s_{\\ell}}",

        "latex": "E[S_{xy}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}E[S_{xy,\\ell}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}[\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})s_\\ell]=L\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_\\ell",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23h",
        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23i",
        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",
        "label": "Formula 20.23i",

        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23j",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",

        "latex": "\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma(w,z)\\\\\\sigma(w,A)\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}S\\\\\\boldsymbol{R}\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma_{z}^{2}&\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma(z,E)\\\\\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma_{A}^{2}&\\sigma(E,A)\\\\\\sigma(z,E)&\\sigma(E,A)&\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}\\beta_{z}\\\\\\beta_{A}\\\\\\beta_{E}\\end{pmatrix}",
        "label": "Formula 20.8a",
        "section": "MODIFYING THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FOR NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Complications in the Absence of Environmental Change",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.8b",
        "latex": "\\sigma(z,A)=\\sigma(A+E+e_{s},A)=\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)",

        "section": "MODIFYING THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FOR NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Complications in the Absence of Environmental Change",
        "position": 18,
        "symbols_defined": [

          "z"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.9a",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}S&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma(z,A)\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(z,E)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\left[\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{A}+\\left[\\sigma_{E}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}+\\sigma(E,A)(\\beta_{A}+\\beta_{E})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.9a",
        "section": "MODIFYING THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FOR NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Complications in the Absence of Environmental Change",
        "position": 19,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "S"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.9b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R&=\\sigma(A,z)\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\left[\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)(\\beta_{z}+\\beta_{E})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.9b",
        "section": "MODIFYING THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FOR NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Complications in the Absence of Environmental Change",

        "symbols_defined": [

        "latex": "\\sigma(z_{1},z_{2})\\beta_{2}=\\sigma(A+E,z_{2})\\beta_{2}=\\sigma(A,z_{2})\\beta_{2}+\\sigma(E,z_{2})\\beta_{2}",

        "latex": "\\sigma(A,z_{2})\\beta_{2}=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}\\qquad\\mathrm{a n d}\\qquad\\sigma(E,z_{2})\\beta_{2}=\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.13a",
        "latex": "h^{2}\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{z}=h^{2}\\left[S-\\left(\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}\\right)\\right]",

        "latex": "R=h^{2}S+\\sigma_{A}^{2}(1-h^{2})\\beta_{A}-h^{2}\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}+\\sigma(E,A)\\left[\\beta_{z}-h^{2}\\beta_{A}+(1-h^{2})\\beta_{E}\\right]",

        "symbols_defined": [

        "latex": "R=\\beta_{z}\\left[\\sigma_{z}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]=\\left(h^{2}+\\frac{\\sigma(E,A)}{\\sigma_{z}^{2}}\\right)S",

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}b_{z}=\\frac{\\sigma(w,z)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)+\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}\\\\&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}h^{2}+\\frac{\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(E)}(1-h^{2})\\\\&=b_{A}h^{2}+b_{E}(1-h^{2})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{x y}=2\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y}=\\frac{2}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}S_{x y,\\ell}",

        "latex": "E[S_{xy}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}E[S_{xy,\\ell}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}[\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})s_\\ell]=L\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_\\ell",

        "latex": "\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y,D W}=\\frac{S_{x y}-\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_{\\ell}}{L-\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_{\\ell}}",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23h",
        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23i",
        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",
        "label": "Formula 20.23i",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23j",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23j",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",

      {

        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{4\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=2\\left[\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\right]=2\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})=2\\theta_{xy}",

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R&=\\sigma(A,z)\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\left[\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)(\\beta_{z}+\\beta_{E})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.9b",
        "section": "MODIFYING THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FOR NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Complications in the Absence of Environmental Change",

        "latex": "h^{2}\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{z}=h^{2}\\left[S-\\left(\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}\\right)\\right]",

        "latex": "R=h^{2}S+\\sigma_{A}^{2}(1-h^{2})\\beta_{A}-h^{2}\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}+\\sigma(E,A)\\left[\\beta_{z}-h^{2}\\beta_{A}+(1-h^{2})\\beta_{E}\\right]",

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}b_{z}=\\frac{\\sigma(w,z)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)+\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}\\\\&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}h^{2}+\\frac{\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(E)}(1-h^{2})\\\\&=b_{A}h^{2}+b_{E}(1-h^{2})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",

      {

        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{x y}=2\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y}=\\frac{2}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}S_{x y,\\ell}",

        "id": "formula_20.23d",
        "latex": "\\widehat{\\theta}_{xy}=\\frac{1}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}\\frac{S_{xy,\\ell}-s_{\\ell}}{1-s_{\\ell}}",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23f",
        "latex": "E[S_{xy}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}E[S_{xy,\\ell}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}[\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})s_\\ell]=L\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_\\ell",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23g",
        "latex": "\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y,D W}=\\frac{S_{x y}-\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_{\\ell}}{L-\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_{\\ell}}",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23h",
        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23h",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23i",
        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",
        "label": "Formula 20.23i",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23j",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23j",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",

        "symbols_defined": [
          "T",
          "\\mathrm{c}",
          "\\mathrm{o}",
          "\\mathrm{r}",
          "x",
          "y"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23k",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{4\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=2\\left[\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\right]=2\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})=2\\theta_{xy}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23k",

        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{x x}=1+\\frac{1}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}\\frac{T_{x,\\ell}^{2}-(1+2p_{\\ell})T_{x,\\ell}+2p_{\\ell}^{2}}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",

        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{xy}=2\\widehat{\\theta}_{xy}=\\frac{1}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}\\frac{\\left(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\quad for x\\neq y",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.24a",
        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}(\\epsilon)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a}-A)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})-2\\sigma(\\widehat{a},A)+\\sigma^{2}(A)=\\sigma^{2}(A)-\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})",
        "label": "Formula 20.24a",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24b",
        "latex": "\\rho=\\frac{\\sigma(\\widehat{a},A)}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(A)}}=\\sqrt{\\frac{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}}",
        "label": "Formula 20.24b",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24c",
        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})=\\rho^{2}\\sigma^{2}(A)",
        "label": "Formula 20.24c",

        "position": 70,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24d",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}\\\\&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(A)\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho}\\geq\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.24d",

        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",

        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",

        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",

        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",

        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{4\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=2\\left[\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\right]=2\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})=2\\theta_{xy}",

        "id": "formula_20.24d",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}\\\\&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(A)\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho}\\geq\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|\\end{aligned}",

        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",

        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",

        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}\\\\&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(A)\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho}\\geq\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|\\end{aligned}",

label: Formula 20.27a
section: APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS
subsection: Bivariate Animal Models: REML Estimates of $ \sigma(A_{z}, A_{w}), S_{A} $, and $ S_{z} $
nearby_text: y_2 = w $ is the vector of corresponding relative fitnesses, so $ (z_i, w_i) $ are the values of the focal trait and relative fitness for individual $ i $. The resulting bivariate mixed model becomes $$ \begin{pmatrix}\mathbf{y}_{1}\\\mathbf{y}_{2}\end{pmatrix}=\begin{pmatrix}\mathbf{z}\\\mathbf{w}\end{pmatrix}=\begin{pmatrix}\mathbf{X}_{z}&\mathbf{0}\\\mathbf{0}&\mathbf{X}_{w}\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}\mathbf{b}_{z}\\\mathbf{b}_{w}\end{pmatrix}+\begin{pmatrix}\mathbf{Z}_{z}&\mathbf{0}\\\mathbf{0}&\mathbf{Z}_{w}\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}\mathbf{a}_{z}\\\mathbf{a}_{w}\end{pmatrix}+\begin{pmatrix}\mathbf{e}_{z}\\\mathbf{e}_{w}\end{pmatrix} $$ (20.27a)

        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",

        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",

        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",

        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{4\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=2\\left[\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\right]=2\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})=2\\theta_{xy}",

        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}(\\epsilon)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a}-A)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})-2\\sigma(\\widehat{a},A)+\\sigma^{2}(A)=\\sigma^{2}(A)-\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.24b",
        "latex": "\\rho=\\frac{\\sigma(\\widehat{a},A)}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(A)}}=\\sqrt{\\frac{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}}",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.24d",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}\\\\&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(A)\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho}\\geq\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|\\end{aligned}",

label: Formula 20.27b
section: APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS
subsection: Bivariate Animal Models: REML Estimates of $ \sigma(A_{z}, A_{w}), S_{A} $, and $ S_{z} $
nearby_text: extended to include more complex designs, such as maternal effects or repeated measures (e.g., Morrissey et al. 2012). The resulting covariance structure for the stacked vector of breeding values is $$ \boldsymbol{\sigma}\left(\mathbf{a}\right)=\boldsymbol{\sigma}\begin{pmatrix}\mathbf{a}_{z}\\ \mathbf{a}_{w}\end{pmatrix}=\begin{pmatrix}\boldsymbol{\sigma}^{2}(A_{z})\mathbf{A}&\boldsymbol{\sigma}(A_{z},A_{w})\mathbf{A}\\ \boldsymbol{\sigma}(A_{z},A_{w})\mathbf{A}&\boldsymbol{\sigma}^{2}(A_{w})\mathbf{A}\end{pmatrix}=\mathbf{G}\otimes\mathbf{A} $$ (20.27b) where $ \otimes $ denotes the Kronecker (or direct) product (LW Chapter 26) and $$ \mathbf{G}=\begin{pmatrix}\sigma^{2}(A_{z})&\sigma(A_{z},A_{w})\\ \sigma(A_{z},A_{w})&\sigma^{2}(A_{w})\end{pmatrix}

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}S&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma(z,A)\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(z,E)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\left[\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{A}+\\left[\\sigma_{E}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}+\\sigma(E,A)(\\beta_{A}+\\beta_{E})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.9a",
        "section": "MODIFYING THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FOR NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Complications in the Absence of Environmental Change",
        "position": 19,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.9b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R&=\\sigma(A,z)\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\left[\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(E,A)(\\beta_{z}+\\beta_{E})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.9b",
        "section": "MODIFYING THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FOR NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Complications in the Absence of Environmental Change",
        "position": 20,

        "latex": "\\sigma(z_{1},z_{2})\\beta_{2}=\\sigma(A+E,z_{2})\\beta_{2}=\\sigma(A,z_{2})\\beta_{2}+\\sigma(E,z_{2})\\beta_{2}",

        "latex": "\\sigma(A,z_{2})\\beta_{2}=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}\\qquad\\mathrm{a n d}\\qquad\\sigma(E,z_{2})\\beta_{2}=\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}",

        "latex": "h^{2}\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}=\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{z}=h^{2}\\left[S-\\left(\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}\\right)\\right]",

        "latex": "R=h^{2}S+\\sigma_{A}^{2}(1-h^{2})\\beta_{A}-h^{2}\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}+\\sigma(E,A)\\left[\\beta_{z}-h^{2}\\beta_{A}+(1-h^{2})\\beta_{E}\\right]",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.19b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}b_{z}=\\frac{\\sigma(w,z)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)+\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(z)}\\\\&=\\frac{\\sigma(w,A)}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}h^{2}+\\frac{\\sigma(w,E)}{\\sigma^{2}(E)}(1-h^{2})\\\\&=b_{A}h^{2}+b_{E}(1-h^{2})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.19b",

      },
      {

      {

        "latex": "\\frac{\\sigma(A_{z},A_{w})+\\sigma(E_{z},e_{w})+\\sigma(A_{z},e_{w})+\\sigma(E_{z},A_{w})}{\\sigma^{2}(A_{z})+\\sigma^{2}(E_{z})}=\\frac{\\sigma(A_{z},A_{w})+\\sigma(A_{z},e_{w})}{\\sigma^{2}(A_{z})}",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.22a",
        "latex": "\\mathbf{y}=\\mathbf{X}\\boldsymbol{\\beta}+\\mathbf{Z}_{a}\\mathbf{a}+\\sum_{i=1}^{k}\\mathbf{Z}_{i}\\mathbf{u}_{i}+\\mathbf{e}",

        "latex": "\\begin{pmatrix}{{{\\mathbf{a}}}} \\\\{{{\\mathbf{e}}}}\\end{pmatrix}\\sim\\begin{pmatrix}{{{\\mathbf{0}}}} \\\\{{{\\mathbf{0}}}}\\end{pmatrix},\\begin{pmatrix}{{{\\sigma^{2}(A)\\mathbf{A}}}}&{{{\\mathbf{0}}}} \\\\{{{\\mathbf{0}}}}&{{{\\sigma_{e}^{2}\\mathbf{I}}}}\\end{pmatrix}",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23b",
        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{x y}=2\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y}=\\frac{2}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}S_{x y,\\ell}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23b",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23c",
        "latex": "E[S_{x y,\\ell}]=\\theta_{x y}+(1-\\theta_{x y})s_{\\ell}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23c",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23d",
        "latex": "\\widehat{\\theta}_{xy}=\\frac{1}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}\\frac{S_{xy,\\ell}-s_{\\ell}}{1-s_{\\ell}}",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23e",
        "latex": "S_{xy}=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}S_{xy,\\ell}",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23f",
        "latex": "E[S_{xy}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}E[S_{xy,\\ell}]=\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}[\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})s_\\ell]=L\\theta_{xy}+(1-\\theta_{xy})\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_\\ell",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23g",
        "latex": "\\widehat{\\theta}_{x y,D W}=\\frac{S_{x y}-\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_{\\ell}}{L-\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}s_{\\ell}}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23g",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23h",
        "latex": "\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)=\\theta_{x y}p_{\\ell}+(1-\\theta_{x y})p_{\\ell}^{2}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23h",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 62,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\Pr",
          "b",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23i",
        "latex": "\\theta_{xy}=\\frac{\\Pr(b_{x,\\ell}=b_{y,\\ell}=1)-p_{\\ell}^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{E[b_{x,\\ell}b_{y,\\ell}]-(E[b_{\\ell}])^{2}}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell})\\sigma(b_{y,\\ell})}=\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})",
        "label": "Formula 20.23i",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 63,
        "symbols_defined": [

          "x",
          "y"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23j",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})}{\\sigma(T_{x,\\ell})\\cdot\\sigma(T_{y,\\ell})}=\\frac{(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell})\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23j",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 64,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "T",
          "\\mathrm{c}",
          "\\mathrm{o}",
          "\\mathrm{r}",
          "x",
          "y"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.23k",
        "latex": "\\mathrm{corr}(T_{x,\\ell},T_{y,\\ell})=\\frac{4\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}=2\\left[\\frac{\\sigma(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})}{p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\right]=2\\mathrm{corr}(b_{x,\\ell},b_{y,\\ell})=2\\theta_{xy}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23k",
        "section": "Selection Response in Natural Populations: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Obtaining the Relationship Matrix: Marker Data",
        "position": 65,
        "symbols_defined": [

      {
        "id": "formula_20.23m",
        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{x x}=1+\\frac{1}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}\\frac{T_{x,\\ell}^{2}-(1+2p_{\\ell})T_{x,\\ell}+2p_{\\ell}^{2}}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}",
        "label": "Formula 20.23m",

      {

        "latex": "\\widehat{A}_{xy}=2\\widehat{\\theta}_{xy}=\\frac{1}{L}\\sum_{\\ell=1}^{L}\\frac{\\left(T_{x,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)\\left(T_{y,\\ell}-2p_{\\ell}\\right)}{2p_{\\ell}(1-p_{\\ell})}\\quad for x\\neq y",

      {
        "id": "formula_20.24a",
        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}(\\epsilon)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a}-A)=\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})-2\\sigma(\\widehat{a},A)+\\sigma^{2}(A)=\\sigma^{2}(A)-\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})",
        "label": "Formula 20.24a",

        "position": 68,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\epsilon",
          "\\sigma"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24b",
        "latex": "\\rho=\\frac{\\sigma(\\widehat{a},A)}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(A)}}=\\sqrt{\\frac{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})}{\\sigma^{2}(A)}}",
        "label": "Formula 20.24b",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Consistency Tests: Accuracy, Reliability, and Caveats when Using PBVs",
        "position": 69,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\rho"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24c",
        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})=\\rho^{2}\\sigma^{2}(A)",
        "label": "Formula 20.24c",
        "section": "APPLYING MIXED MODELS TO NATURAL POPULATIONS",

        "position": 70,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\sigma",
          "\\widehat{a}"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.24d",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(\\widehat{a},z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(\\widehat{a})\\sigma^{2}(z)}}\\\\&=\\frac{\\left|\\sigma(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho\\sqrt{\\sigma^{2}(A)\\sigma^{2}(z)}}=\\frac{\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|}{\\rho}\\geq\\left|\\operatorname{corr}(A,z)\\right|\\end{aligned}",

      {

      {
        "id": "formula_20.7b",
        "latex": "\\sigma(A,w\\mid\\mid z)=\\sigma(E,w\\mid\\mid z)=0",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.8a",
        "latex": "\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma(w,z)\\\\\\sigma(w,A)\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}S\\\\\\boldsymbol{R}\\\\\\sigma(w,E)\\end{pmatrix}=\\begin{pmatrix}\\sigma_{z}^{2}&\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma(z,E)\\\\\\sigma(z,A)&\\sigma_{A}^{2}&\\sigma(E,A)\\\\\\sigma(z,E)&\\sigma(E,A)&\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}\\beta_{z}\\\\\\beta_{A}\\\\\\beta_{E}\\end{pmatrix}",
        "label": "Formula 20.8a",
        "section": "MODIFYING THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FOR NATURAL POPULATIONS",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.8b",
        "latex": "\\sigma(z,A)=\\sigma(A+E+e_{s},A)=\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_20.9a",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}S&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma(z,A)\\beta_{A}+\\sigma(z,E)\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\left[\\sigma_{A}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{A}+\\left[\\sigma_{E}^{2}+\\sigma(E,A)\\right]\\beta_{E}\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\beta_{z}+\\sigma_{A}^{2}\\beta_{A}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}\\beta_{E}+\\sigma(E,A)(\\beta_{A}+\\beta_{E})\\\\ \\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 20.9a",
        "section": "MODIFYING THE BREEDER'S EQUATION FOR NATURAL POPULATIONS",
        "subsection": "Complications in the Absence of Environmental Change",
        "position": 19,
        "symbols_defined": [
